content:
I need to multiply a4a × 291 in base 11.

First, let me convert each base-11 number to base-10:
- a4a in base 11 = 10×11² + 4×11¹ + 10×11⁰ = 10×121 + 4×11 + 10×1 = 1210 + 44 + 10 = 1264 (base 10)
- 291 in base 11 = 2×11² + 9×11¹ + 1×11⁰ = 2×121 + 9×11 + 1×1 = 242 + 99 + 1 = 342 (base 10)

Now multiply in base 10:
1264 × 342 = 432,288 (base 10)

Now convert 432,288 back to base 11:
432288 ÷ 11 = 39298 remainder 10 (digit: 10 = a)
39298 ÷ 11 = 3572 remainder 6 (digit: 6)
3572 ÷ 11 = 324 remainder 8 (digit: 8)
324 ÷ 11 = 29 remainder 5 (digit: 5)
29 ÷ 11 = 2 remainder 7 (digit: 7)
2 ÷ 11 = 0 remainder 2 (digit: 2)

Reading the remainders in reverse: 27586a

27586a
